channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Fortnite's return to the Apple App Store and iOS has caused an eight-year record surge in downloads, with a spike in daily activity, too
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has faced tons of difficulties in recent times, with engagement going down and massive layoffs at Epic affecting its short-term creative potential. However, its recent return to the Apple App Store has kickstarted a surge in downloads and activity.

The legal battle with Apple over storefront taxes and payment options isn't over yet, but the raw numbers are in following Fortnite's global return to iOS devices, and they're fantastic to the point it almost neared its 2018 launch-week popularity on Apple's platform.

GamesIndustry.biz shared the data by AppMagic and compared the numbers to other key moments of Fortnite's long life. With 3.4m downloads during last week's return to the App Store, it nearly matched the launch-week figure of 3.7m, landing "its fourth-strongest week" ever. If you were wondering why Epic had been fighting Apple so hard over the game's presence on Apple devices, there's your answer.

Moreover, the global relaunch of sorts caused a sharp increase in daily activity, with installs rising by 1,408 percent, "from approximately 19,000 on May 18 to nearly 290,000 on May 19, peaking at 674,000 on May 23." For reference, the original all-time record was around 764k daily downloads when it first launched on iOS. The previous daily install peak of 569k - achieved on 24th May, 2025, after its return to iOS only in the US - was also crushed thanks to the game reaching a global audience again.

Is this enough to revitalise Fortnite in a way that satisfies Epic CEO Tim Sweeney and his team? It's too early to tell, but it won't bring back the hundreds of employees that were recently laid off. While the company's plans remain ambitious and might potentially extend beyond Fortnite, the future is looking murky at best.

Mentioned in this article

Fortnite

Android, Mac, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2, PC, PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S, iOS



https://www.eurogamer.net/fortnite-apple-app-store-ios-return
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bungie details Marathon Season 2 and the new Sentinel class, with a free-to-play week and special sale planned, too
item.content:encoded:
Right after dropping the untimely news Destiny 2 won't be getting more support after June, Bungie is ramping up work on Marathon, with Season 2 introducing a spooky new map and mysterious new threats next week.

Alongside the terrifying cinematic trailer, the developers have now detailed everything we should expect from Marathon Season 2, at least during its early stages. The 2nd June update will mark the beginning of the game's second season, and an 'Open Play Week' - ending on 9th June - is inviting all curious players who want to experience what Tau Ceti IV has to offer.

"During the Open Play Week, the full game will be available to play on Steam, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S, and any progress you make will carry over into Season 2. This will be a great time to invite your friends to crew up as everyone will be starting out on a clean slate," the blog post explained.

The 'Night' variant of the Dire Marsh map intends to keep veterans and newcomers alike on their toes, with blocked sight lines and substantially darker environments making every fight more potentially lethal. New mechanics, enemies, and locations will be introduced, with a focus on a slower pace and survival horror-like vibes. New equipment - flashlights, vector rounds and grenades, darksight scopes, and flares - will help Runners navigate (and fight in) the dark.

The other key addition is the Sentinel shell, Marathon's first post-launch class, which is built for "controlling space and defending areas" with a Defender System which destroys all incoming grenades and missiles while boosting nearby crewmates. Their tactical ability is a snare mine which can immobilise enemy Runners. And last but not least, their passives improve damage resistance versus splash damage, faster weapon handling of close-range weapons, and even motion-tracking of "nearby moving targets".

Two new weapons - the KKV-9SD SMG and D54 Battle Pistol - are also arriving with Season 2, though it's unclear if they'll be available day one. The former has an extremely fast rate of fire, while the latter is good for "1v1 duels" with three-round bursts. New mods and chips are being added as well.

Perhaps the more substantial addition to the game will be The Cradle, which introduces a totally new way to "climb" the season as Bungie seeks to reinvent progression with each reset. "With the Cradle, you'll empower the WEAVEworms that create your biocybernetic bodies to increase your Runner shell stats across six categories and unlock powerful perks," the post explained. It all works thanks to the Matter Converter, which turns loot you don't want into Cradle experience. Cradle "energy" can be reallocated with no penalties and is shared across all shells.

The Duos queue - previously tested but not fully implemented - will have a bigger presence in this season too, with Ranked kicking off on 14th June and the high-level Cryo Archive zone set to return on 11th June. As you can tell, Bungie is betting hard and fast on making Marathon a far more varied and appealing extraction shooter experience, but PvE-first players will have to wait for a bit longer still.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/marathon-season-2-rundown
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve announces significant Steam Deck price rise of almost £200 in the UK, over 40% around the world
item.content:encoded:
"These new prices reflect the current state of component costs and other global logistical challenges."

Valve has announced a substantial price increase for its Steam Deck OLED models, citing "rising memory and storage costs" as the reason.

In the UK, Valve's lower-tier 512GB Steam Deck OLED model has increased from £479 to £649. Meanwhile, the chunkier 1TB Steam Deck OLED model will increase from £569 to £779. Pricings for other regions can be found below.

  • Steam Deck OLED 512GB: Now $789 USD; CAD 1,129; EUR 779; AUD 1,199; PLN 3,279
  • Steam Deck OLED 1TB: Now $949 USD; CAD 1,349; EUR 919; AUD 1,429; PLN 3,879

Valve stated the Steam Deck itself has not changed, and rather "these new prices reflect the current state of component costs and other global logistical challenges across the industry as a whole".

The price of RAM - the quick access source of memory in a computer - has skyrocketed over recent months. In fact, back in November of last year, some shops stopped printing price labels so they could alter the prices daily. This rising cost has led to memory shortages, and in April, head of Xbox Asha Sharma admitted this could in turn lead to a price bump and limited stock where its next-gen console, Project Helix, is concerned. Meanwhile, in February, Valve was forced to revise its plans for its Steam Machine and Steam Frame hardware due to global memory and storage shortages caused by massive demand from tech giants going all-in on AI data centres.

Valve isn't the only company to have increased the price of its current hardware recently. In February, Sony announced a shock price rise for its PlayStation 5 family of consoles, citing "continued pressures in the global economic landscape" as the reason for the changes. Earlier this month, Nintendo also announced a price increase for the Switch 2, and similarly said the "recent surge in memory and other component prices" was a major factor in its decision.

As for the future, Valve has still not set a retail price for its Steam Machine, and there are now concerns within the gaming community about its affordability. The Steam Machine is a console/PC hybrid which will be able to run PC games on a television or as a normal gaming computer. In November, Valve's Lawrence Yang and Pierre-Loup Griffais briefly discussed the pricing, and said the Steam Machine will be "more in line with what you might expect from [the] current PC market". Given how much the company's handheld Steam Deck now costs, though, it is safe to say that its console-like device will not come cheap.

For more on this subject, be sure to check out Bertie's feature: "It's a perfect storm of unpreparedness, panic, and greed" - RAM prices are soaring, so what does it mean for gaming?

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/steam-deck-price-increase
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
